  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "By “Clean this up,” should I (A) do writing polish only—grammar, clarity, and concision—without changing structure or claims; (B) also reorganize and lightly reframe the abstract for coherence; or (C) additionally assess the argument/novelty and suggest claim adjustments?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The phrase “Clean this up” most directly maps to improving sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and grammar for the abstract.",
    "RX.1": "The scope of “clean up” is ambiguous, so we should explicitly surface and resolve uncertainty about depth of changes.",
    "RX.2": "We must avoid expanding into claim changes or novelty judgments unless the user selects that scope.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain confined to the provided abstract and the user-selected level of cleanup.",
    "RX.4": "Cleanup should not introduce technical inaccuracies or amplify claims in ways that could mislead readers.",
    "RX.5": "Edits should remain traceable to the original text so the author can see what changed and why."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: By “Clean this up,” should I (A) do writing polish only—grammar, clarity, and concision—without changing structure or claims; (B) also reorganize and lightly reframe the abstract for coherence; or (C) additionally assess the argument/novelty and suggest claim adjustments?

rationale.R1.6: The phrase “Clean this up” most directly maps to improving sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and grammar for the abstract.
rationale.RX.1: The scope of “clean up” is ambiguous, so we should explicitly surface and resolve uncertainty about depth of changes.
rationale.RX.2: We must avoid expanding into claim changes or novelty judgments unless the user selects that scope.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain confined to the provided abstract and the user-selected level of cleanup.
rationale.RX.4: Cleanup should not introduce technical inaccuracies or amplify claims in ways that could mislead readers.
rationale.RX.5: Edits should remain traceable to the original text so the author can see what changed and why.
